title: The One With The Baby Shower

lines.text: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Phoebe are preparing for Rachel's baby shower.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what's the final head count on my baby shower?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: About twenty, a couple people from work who had something else to do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Also both of your sisters called and neither can make it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! You mean they're not coming to a social event where there's no men and there's no booze?! That's shocking! I don't care, as long as my mom's here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, your mother!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! My mom's not gonna be here?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, given that we forgot to invite her it would be an awfully big coincidence if she was.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well it wasn't my fault, Phoebe was in charge of the invitations!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well I don't, I don't have a mother so often I forget that other people...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting her) Oh give it a rest!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So my mother is not coming to my baby shower?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. (Pause) Neither is mine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, y'know what? Don't worry, okay? We'll take care of it. We'll call her. Just go home and get ready.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Please, make sure she comes. It's really important to me, I mean it's my mom!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. I know, what's her number?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Go! I have it in my book. Go! (Rachel leaves and Monica calls Mrs. Green.) (To Phoebe) Wait a minute! If you're in charge of the invitations why am I the one who has to call her-Hello Mrs. Green! Hi, it's Monica Geller.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh, hello Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone) Hi, umm I know this is last minute, but we've decided to throw an impromptu baby shower for Rachel today.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: I know, my daughter's told me about it when they received their impromptu invitations a month ago.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sorry. I'm-I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: For what dear? For not inviting me or lying about it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Oh my God, my ass is sweating! (on phone) Please! Please! Can you come? It's today at four.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well all right. I'll see you at four.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. (Hangs up.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Isn't it at three?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Son of a bitch! (Calls Mrs. Green again.)
Opening Credits
Joey's Apartment, Joey is reading a script as Ross and Chandler enter carrying a basketball.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe! You wanna shoot some hoops?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no, I can't go. I'm practicing; I got an audition to be the host of a new game show.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh cool!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, and if I get it by day I'll (In a sexy voice) Dr. Drake Remoray, but by night I'll be (In an announcer's voice) Joey Trrrribbiani!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You'll be perfect for this! That's already your name!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But the audition's in a couple hours and I don't even understand the game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well do you want some help?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh really? That'd be great! You guys can be the contestants!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Awesome!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I guess we can lose to junior high girls some other time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (announcer voice) All right! Let's play Bamboozled!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bamboozled?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, isn't that a cool name?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (simultaneously with Chandler) Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (simultaneously with Ross) No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Uhh, okay. Our first contestant is Ross Geller. Why don't you tell us a little something about you Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well uh, I-I'm a paleontologist. Umm, I-I live in New York. I have a son Ben. Uh, hi Ben! (Waves.) And uh...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I said a little bit Ross. Now, how about you Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well Joey, I'm a headhunter. I hook up out of work Soviet scientists with rogue third-world nations. Hi Rasputin! (Waves.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Excellent! Let's play Bamboozled! Chandler, you'll go first. What is the capital of Columbia?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bogota.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's Ba-go-ta, but close enough. Now, you can either pass your turn to Ross or pick a Wicked Wango card.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What does a Wicked Wango card do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I should know that. Let's see, just one moment please. Umm, here we are, a Wicked Wango card determines whether you go higher or lower.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Higher or lower than what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is embarrassing. (Looks it up.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) Can you believe how lame this is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't believe contestants are allowed to talk to each other.
Monica and Chandler's, Rachel's baby shower is underway. Monica and Phoebe are working in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I told the stripper to be here at five. That's good right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You ordered a stripper for the shower?! That is totally inappropriate!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? He's gonna be dressed as a baby! (Mrs. Green enters.) Oh hi Mrs. Green!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm so glad you could make it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, thank you so much. And again, we're so sorry. We could not feel worse about it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Try. There's my little girl. (Goes over to Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She's still mad.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I know. Isn't it great? One less person we have to make small talk with.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, Sandra's mad at you too. It-it doesn't bother you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No look, we've apologized twice! I can't do anymore than that. I know you hate it when people are mad at you but you just have to be okay with it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I can do that. (Pause) I gotta go powder my ass.
[Cut to Rachel and Mrs. Green.]

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Look at that face! Just like when you were in high school! If I didn't know better I'd say you were a cheerleader in trouble. Come on, let's get some tea.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (Mrs. Green helps her up and they walk over and get some tea.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh my look at that. Only three weeks to go, now have you picked your nanny yet? Now I don't want you to use your housekeeper 'cause it would just split her focus.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh well actually gonna use a nanny and uh, I don't even have a housekeeper.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: It's like you're a cave person. Rachel, you must get a nanny. You don't know how overwhelming this is going to be. I mean when you were a baby I had full time help, I had Mrs. Kay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mrs. Kay! Oh yeah, she was sweet. She taught me Spanish. I actually think I remember some of it, tu madre es loca. (I think that's your mother's crazy.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Such a sweet woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, however great she was I just can't afford that.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: I just had the greatest idea! I'm gonna come live with you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wh-wh-what? What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh, I'm so happy I'm gonna do this for my little girl. Aw, look at you. You have tears in your eyes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes. Yes I do.
Joey's Apartment, the guys are still playing Bamboozled.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right Ross you're in the lead, would you like to take another question or spin the Wheel of Mayhem?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The wheel has not been my friend tonight Joey. Uh, I'll take another question.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, this is gonna be tough. Hold your breath.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's okay, I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No dude, you gotta hold your breath until you're ready to answer the question.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is ridiculous, he's not gonna hold his breath... (Ross cuts him off by taking a deep breath and holding it.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, what do you have a fear of if you suffer from this phobia, Tris...Holy cow, that's a big word. Trisc... Seriously look at this thing. Chandler, how do you say that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me see that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This one right here. (Ross whines.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Triscadecaphobia.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (exhaling) The fear of Triscuts!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No, fear of the number 13.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fear of Triscuts?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's possible, they have really sharp edges.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right Chandler, you're up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, I-I believe I'm entitled to use my Angel Pass for a free turn?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This game makes no sense!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what? You're just upset because you're losing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on Ross, I think we're all losers here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Chandler, you can either spin the wheel or pick a Google card.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me think. Let me think-Oh! I don't care.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You-you must choose Mr. Bing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Either, it makes no difference.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Choose, you jackass!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll take a card.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, you picked the Gimmie card! You get all of Ross's points!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This game is kinda fun.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Chandler) You don't think it's a little crazy that you get all my points just 'cause you...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think the contestants are supposed to speak to each other.
Monica and Chandler's, the baby shower is continuing as Rachel walks over to Monica and Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why did you invite my mother?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She wants to move in with me and Ross to help take care of the baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: For how long?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Eight weeks. I mean I love my mother, but my God, a long lunch with her is taxing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I personally would be honored if she wanted to live with me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She can't hear you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? You guys, come on! What am I going to do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if you don't want your mother to move in with you, just tell her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're right. You're right. I mean I'm about to have a baby, I can tell my mother that I don't want her to just be sleeping on my couch! Oh my God! She's gonna want to sleep in my bed with me. This cannot happen!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right. That is right, you go over there and tell her you don't want her to live with you. Do not take no for an answer!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (She goes over to tell her mother.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Phoebe) This is great! Now she's gonna be mad at Rachel! Y'know what? And I'm just gonna swoop in there and be like the daughter she never had.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have new respect for Chandler. All right everybody! It's time to open the presents!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Yes! And I think that the first gift that Rachel opens should be from the grandmother of the baby, because you're the most important person in this room. And in the world!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well uh, I don't have a gift because I wasn't invited until the last minute, but thank you so much for bringing that to everyone's attention.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How about you less important people, let's open your presents!
Mrs. Green goes into the kitchen and Rachel follows her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mom that's okay that you didn't get you a gift!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well, I kinda did. Me. Eight weeks of me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. Okay, see mom, the truth is I can do this on my own.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Sweetheart I know you're gonna be terrific mom, I just think you need a little help, especially at the beginning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But mom, I really know what I'm doing. I can handle this.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Really? Remember Twinkles?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He was a hamster! I am not going to vacuum up my baby!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, come on Rach it's present time! Y'know you're the glue that's holding this whole party together. It's kinda falling apart here.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh look.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this is from your friend at work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my gosh! Oh wow! Oh, I know what this is! (She's holding an item with a large suction cup connected to a yellow plastic box, with a long narrow tube and bottle connected the yellow part.) Wait a minute. That can't be right. Is that a beer bong for a baby?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Darling, that's a breast pump!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did I say I was done guessing? Okay, thank you for that. Oh wow! What's this?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: It's a diaper genie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it dispenses clean diapers!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: No! It's where you put the dirty ones!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that's gross, why don't you just take it outside and throw it in a dumpster?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh you're gonna do that ten times a day?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! It goes ten times a day! What are we feeding this baby?! Indian food?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: No dear, that's what babies do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, listen to your mother. She is very smart.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Plus, what are you planning on doing with the baby while you're trotting out to the garbage ten times a day?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, I'd leave it on the changing table? (Everyone gasps.) What?! What'd I do? What'd I do?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: You can't leave a baby alone!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh come-(Stutters)-Of course I know that. I mean of course you never leave a baby alone! I mean who would-she wouldn't be safe as she would be with me, the baby dummy. Oh God, okay. Y'know what? I think opening the presents right now is a little overwhelming right now. So I think umm, I'm just gonna maybe open them a little bit later, but thank you all for coming. And for these beautiful gifts, and this basket is beautiful.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: It's actually a bassinet.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay mommy, don't ever leave me. (Hugs her.)
Commercial Break
Joey's Apartment, the guys are still playing the game only everyone is really into it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Chandler) In what John Houston film would you hear this line, "Badges? We don't need no stinkin' badges!"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Treasure of the Sierra Madre!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct! There's a possible backwards bonus!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Madre Sierra the of Treasure!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd like to go up the ladder of chance to the golden mud hut please.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wise choice, how many rungs?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Six!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (makes a sound like a monkey) That noise can only me one thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (disappointed and simultaneously as Ross) Hungry monkey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (excited and simultaneously as Chandler) Hungry monkey! (To Chandler) Haaa! (To Joey) I'd like a Wicked Wango card!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, it's an audio question, name this television theme song. (Starts humming the theme to I Dream of Genie.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (thinking) Oh. (Pause) Oh! Oh my God! Okay, I know this, give me-give me a second!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tell it to the Time Turtle!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shut up! I Dream of Genie!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Yes, you're back in the lead!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'd like to spin the wheel!
Joey makes a sound like a game show wheel spinning with the pointer bouncing off of the bars on the wheel as it slows and comes to a stop.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (annoyed) Oh come on!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! All right! Uh, umm, Super-Speedy Speed round!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is there a hopping bonus?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course!
Ross gets up and starts to hop on one leg.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who invented bifocals?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ben Franklin.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct! Which monarch has ruled Great Britain the longest?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Queen Victoria.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct again! But, you forgot to switch legs between questions, so no hopping bonus!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Noooo!!! Every time!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now, over to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'd like a Google Card.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! (Pause) No! (Pause) Google!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! Congratulations Ross, because Chandler, you've been Bamboozled!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nooo!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is the best game ever!!!
Monica and Chandler's, the baby shower has ended and everyone except for Mrs. Green have left who is talking to Rachel while Monica and Phoebe are cleaning up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So umm, you're gonna stay with me as long as I need you?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Of course I am!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh mom, I swear I'm not an idiot. I've read all kinds of books on pregnancy and giving birth, but I-I just didn't think to read the part about what to do when the baby comes. And-and then guess what? The baby's coming and I don't know what to do. Oh, can I throw up in my diaper genie?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: No. Sweetie, you're gonna be fine. (Starts to get up.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait-wait where are you going? Where are you going?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: I'm going to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Now don't worry! Everything's gonna be okay. (Hugs Rachel while she is standing and Rachel is sitting, seeing this Monica decides to join in on the hugging by hugging Mrs. Green from behind her back.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It is going to be okay! (Mrs. Green glances over her shoulder and glares at Monica while she heads for the bathroom.) It was worth a shot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering, out of breath) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Why are you all red and sweaty?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just Bamboozled Chandler! (Flexes in victory while everyone stares at him.) Which is not uh sexual thing. That was a quick shower.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not if you were here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! It looks like we got a lot of good stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh we did, but my mom got us the greatest gift of all.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (excited) A Play-Dough Barber Shop?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. She's going to live with us for eight weeks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! She's gonna help us take care of the baby! Woo-hoo. (Sees that Ross isn't happy.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What-You're not serious. I mean she's a very nice woman, but there is no way we can take eight weeks of her. She'll drive us totally crazy.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: (entering from bathroom) Hi Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi roomie! (Hugs her and looks at Rachel.)
Joey's Audition, Joey is being shown in.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Hey Joey, hi! I'm Ray; I'm the producer of the show.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (announcer voice) It's a pleasure to meet you Ray.

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: And this is Duncan (points to the cameraman) and Erin, they're gonna help us out with the audition. So uh, let's get the camera rolling.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (announcer voice) Rightie-O Ray!

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Whenever you're ready.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to the camera) Hello, I'm Joey Tribbiani! Let's play Bamboozled! Erin, you get the first question! In hockey, who is known as The Great One?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Wayne Gretzky.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct! Now, would you like to pick a Wicked Wango card or spin the Wheel of Mayhem?

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Uh Joey, didn't your agents give you the revised rules? We've eliminated all of that. No wheel, no cards.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-Why?!

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Uh well, the game was too complicated and research showed people didn't follow it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well what's complicated? You spin the Wheel of Mayhem to go up the Ladder of Chance. You go past the Mud Hut through the Rainbow Ring to get to the Golden Monkey; you yank his tail and boom! You're in Paradise Pond!

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Yeah all that's gone. It's basically just a simple question and answer game now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well what's fun about that? You expect me to be the host of a boring game that's just people standing around answering questions?

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Well, there'll be women in bikinis holding up the scores.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (announcer voice to the camera) Let's play Bamboozled!
Monica and Chandler's, Mrs. Green is telling Ross what needs to be done to baby proof his apartment.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: ...and all those dinosaur nick-knacks you have Ross, I thought they might be more at home in the garage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well we...we don't have a garage.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Did I say garage? I meant garbage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what? Maybe, Mrs. Green, it's not absolutely vital that you live with us.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well Rachel needs help with the baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I do. I really do. I don't know anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm-I'm sure that's not true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no? Pheebs? Monica? Do I know anything about babies?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, not a thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's frightening.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well uh, y'know what? Even if she doesn't know anything, I do! I have a son. And his mother and I didn't live together, and whenever he was with me I took care of him all the time, by myself.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: That's true. You do have another child.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: With another woman. Have you no control Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's a different issue. Uh, the point is, when the baby comes I will be there to...to feed her and bathe her and change her. And more than that I want to do all those things.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well then you really don't need me to live with you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! Yes, you're gonna be so missed.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: You're gonna be a great father.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well you're gonna be a wonderful grandma. (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello?! I still don't know what the hell I'm doing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on, every first time mother feels that way. You'll-you're gonna pick it up. (Rachel doesn't believe that.) Hey! You will! Uh look, y'know when you first came to the city? You were this spoiled helpless little girl who-who still used daddy's credit card. Do you remember?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I hope you're going somewhere with this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at you! What-You're-you're this big executive! You are much more capable than you give yourself credit for. I-I have no doubt you're gonna be an incredible mother.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm telling you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. (Hugs him.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: All right you two, I'm gonna get going.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. (Rachel and he start to stand up.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no sweetheart, you stay put. I'll let myself out. It's like I'm not here, which I almost wasn't.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) You're still so funny. You're so funny. (To Phoebe) What do I do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing! You have apologized to her like a million times and she's been nothing but terrible to you. And don't forget you just threw her daughter a lovely, albeit slightly boring, shower, and she hasn't even thanked you for it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what? You're-you're right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I mean if you want to say anything to her, I'd tell her off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! I will! Mrs. Green? Mrs. Green! (She ignores Monica and Monica follows her out into the hall with Phoebe in tow.) It is rude to leave a party without saying good-bye to the host! Yeah, and-and also when someone apologizes to you the decent thing to do is to accept it! Now what I did to you, it wasn't on purpose! But what you're during to me now is just plain spiteful!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Spiteful?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right! Maybe it's time you took a good hard look at a mirror young lady...old lady...lady!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Monica) Wrap it up, wrap it up, wrap it up...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So whenever you're ready to apologize to me, I will forgive you. Good day! (Monica and Phoebe reenter the apartment and Monica closes the door on a stunned Mrs. Green.) I can't feel my legs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You were fantastic! I'm so proud of you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah? I'm proud of me too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You should be!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah could-could-could you get me something to drink?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You got it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (When Phoebe turns around Monica runs out into the hall after Mrs. Green.) Mrs. Green! Okay I'm really sorry!! I'm apologizing for the-(She trips and falls down the stairs.) (Pause) Okay, I bit my tongue, but I'm still really sorry!
Closing Credits
Ross and Rachel's, Ross is helping Rachel study for when the baby comes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (closing a book) Okay! I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I've done my studying and I really know my stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right then. (Gets up, in an announcer's voice) Rachel Green! Let's play Bamboozled! (Reading from a note card.) How do you test the temperature of the baby's bath water?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, put your elbow in it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excellent! How do you put a baby down for a nap?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Full, dry, on its back, and no loose covers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's correct! This is an audio question, what do you do when the baby makes this sound? (Makes a sound like someone is choking a cat.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Check if it's wet, check if it's hungry, burp it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excellent! Excellent, now-now do you want another question or a Wicked Wango card?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A card! A card! I pick a card!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry you've been Bamboozled! You're gonna be a terrible mother! (Rachel stares at him agape.) I've lost sight of why we're doing this! (Rachel gets up and walks away.)
End
